resources.string: Action recorded (will be taken at next cycle).
Name and bundle are both required.
Invalid bundle — paste the exact 128-character public key shared by your peer.
Fingerprint: (paste the recipient\'s public key above)
From %s — tap to open the chat
evolIA stopped — messaging inactive (messages are erased on stop).
Owner auth not configured yet — reopen evolIA and complete the PIN/password setup first.

Choose a transfer mode:

• On-chain (recommended): secure, final, blockchain-settled, requires RPC
• Local P2P (at-risk): peer promise, offline-friendly, no blockchain needed

This is an off-chain, non-binding transfer. You are making a peer promise to send BTC-e, but it is NOT blockchain-settled. The receiver must trust you. The mesh network can detect and contain malicious double-spends via adaptive defense, but this record is local only.\n\nProceed at your risk.
Recorded local promise: %1$.2f BTC-e to %2$s. The mesh can verify claims if shared.
Add a chat contact first — an offline transfer is carried over the encrypted mesh to a known peer.
Share this address — peers transfer BTC-e to it and your balance updates automatically on-chain:\n\n%1$s
Invalid RPC URL — it must start with https:// or http:// and have a host.
Invalid chain_id — it must be a positive whole number.

Your BTC-e is the value evolIA builds from your activity. \"On-chain\" means a transfer is written on a blockchain — a shared, tamper-proof ledger — so it cannot be faked or spent twice. Exchanging on-chain needs three things, explained below: a connection to the blockchain (RPC), a tiny network fee (gas), and the recipient\'s address. New to crypto? Read on — each step is explained plainly.
1. What is an RPC, and how to set it

A blockchain runs on many computers called nodes. An RPC is simply the web address (a URL) of one such node — the door the app knocks on to read balances and send transfers. You also give a chain_id, a number that identifies which network you're on. You obtain both OUTSIDE the app (a phone web browser, or a PC), then place them in evolIA's config. Here is how, step by step.

Option A — a public test network (fastest, no account):
1. In your phone's web browser, open chainlist.org.
2. Turn on "Testnets" and search a test network (e.g. Sepolia).
3. It lists ready-to-use RPC URLs and shows the chain_id (Sepolia = 11155111).
4. Copy one RPC URL and the chain_id.

Option B — a free RPC provider (steadier, free sign-up):
1. In your browser, open a free provider (e.g. Alchemy, Infura, or Ankr).
2. Create a free account (email + password), then a new app/project.
3. Pick the network; you get an https:// RPC URL — copy it.
4. The chain_id is the one of the network you picked (check chainlist.org if unsure).

Option C — a local node among friends (no internet, full control):
1. On a PC on the same Wi-Fi, run a free local node (e.g. Ganache).
2. Your RPC URL is http:// + the PC's local IP + :8545 (e.g. http://192.168.1.20:8545).
3. The chain_id is the node's id (Ganache's default is 1337).

Where evolIA reads them: the file evolia_chain_config.json holds rpc_url and chain_id, e.g. {"rpc_url": "https://…", "chain_id": 11155111}. On Termux it lives in your evolia folder. On Android, tap "On-chain settings (RPC)" and paste the RPC URL and chain_id there. If it's empty or missing, evolIA stays in LOCAL mode and nothing settles on-chain. Tip: start on a test network — it behaves exactly the same, with zero risk.
2. What \"gas\" is, and how to fund it

Every blockchain charges a small fee, called gas, to process a transaction. Gas is paid in the network's own coin (its "native coin"). You only ever need a tiny amount.

Two situations:
• Test network (recommended to start): gas is free. Local accounts come pre-funded, or a free "faucet" hands you test coins. No bank, no card, no money.
• Real public network: the native coin costs real money, so you buy a small amount and send it to your address (shown at the bottom of this page).

How to get the coin with real money: open an account on an exchange such as Binance or OKX, then top it up — in Madagascar this is commonly done with Mobile Money (MVola, Orange Money, Airtel Money) through the exchange's P2P market, or with a Visa/Mastercard card, or a bank transfer. Buy a small amount of the network's native coin, then withdraw it to your evolIA address. IMPORTANT: pick the matching network when withdrawing — sending on the wrong network loses the funds.
3. Sending, receiving, and using Binance / OKX

Good to know: BTC-e lives inside evolIA's contract and moves directly between evolIA users. It is NOT a coin listed on Binance or OKX — you cannot send BTC-e to an exchange. Exchanges are only used to obtain the gas coin that funds your address.

To send: ask the other person for their evolIA address (their Receive screen shows a 0x… address). Tap Transfer, paste the address and the amount, and confirm with your PIN/password. The blockchain settles it and it can never overspend.

To receive: tap Receive and share your 0x… address. When someone transfers to it, your balance updates on its own — receiving is automatic, nothing to do.

If you use Binance/OKX: that's where you withdraw the gas coin to your evolIA address (a standard 0x… address, on the matching network). The BTC-e exchange itself still happens between evolIA users, inside the app.

• Your private key stays encrypted on this phone — never share it, your PIN, or your password.
• Always double-check the address and the network before sending. Transfers are final.
• Begin on a test network with people you trust: same experience, no money at risk.
• Keep only a little gas — fees are tiny.

For peer messaging, turn on Bluetooth and Wi-Fi so your messages can reach nearby peers. Bluetooth works with no internet.\n\nIMPORTANT: turning Bluetooth on is not enough — you must also PAIR the two phones in Bluetooth settings (Pair new device). Without OS-level pairing, evolIA cannot reach the peer over Bluetooth.
For offline peer messaging, turn on Bluetooth so your messages can reach nearby peers without internet.
Turn on Wi-Fi so your messages can reach peers on your local network.

RADIO
• Bluetooth: %1$s
• BT permission: %2$s
• Wi-Fi: %5$s

PAIRED DEVICES (%3$d):
%4$s

SEND COUNTERS (since service start)
• Frames sent: %6$d
• Connect attempts: %7$d
• Connect successes: %8$d

RECEIVE COUNTERS (since service start)
• Accepted connections: %9$d
• Frames received: %10$d
• Intake rejections: %11$d

OUTBOX: %12$d message(s) pending

HOW TO READ THIS:
• If "Connect attempts" stays at 0, no peer was tried (no bonded device, or radio off).
• If "attempts > 0" but "successes = 0", the paired device is not running evolIA (or you paired a car/headset, not the other phone).
• If "successes > 0" but the receiver shows "Frames received = 0", the connection drops before any frame goes through (range issue).
• If receiver shows "Frames received > 0" but message not displayed, check fingerprint match (sender's contact fingerprint must equal receiver's identity).

WI-FI / UDP TRANSPORT (since service start)
• Sends OK: %1$d
• Sends failed: %2$d
• Cold peers: %3$d / %4$d known
• Throttle events:
 - Egress (self-DoS guard): %5$d
 - Ingress defense gate: %6$d
 - Cold-peer skipped: %7$d
• Receives: blocks=%8$d, chat=%9$d
• Attacks (blocks): inj=%10$d, badsig=%11$d, forged=%12$d, malformed=%13$d
• Attacks (chat): inj=%14$d, malformed=%15$d
• Defense levels (separate intakes):
 - Blocks: %16$.2f
 - Chat: %17$.2f
• Live cycle: %18$d ms (base %19$d ms — adaptive stretch under pressure, up to 2×)

HOW TO READ THIS (UDP):
• If "Sends OK" stays at 0, mesh-sync is not reaching any peer (binary not running, or no peer in evolia_peers.json).
• If "Sends OK > 0" but the receiver shows "Receives chat = 0", the datagrams arrive but the recipient fingerprint does not match yours (or the receiver's mesh-sync is silent).
• "Cold peers > 0" means N peers are in the exponential-backoff window (5s → 5 min) after repeated failures — they are not being dialled until the window expires.
• Throttle events: "Egress" is self-shaping, "Ingress defense" is reactive to hostile pressure, "Cold-peer skipped" means peer-health saved a wasted Dial.
• Defense levels: with flow isolation, blocks and chat are throttled independently. A chat attack does not starve block propagation, and vice versa. The cycle stretches by the max of the two levels.
• "Live cycle > base cycle" means the loop is stretched because one or both defenses absorbed pressure — battery saving while under attack. Returns to base automatically as both buffers decay.
To keep accumulating value in the background, allow evolIA to run without battery restrictions. It keeps running until you press Stop.
Move and orient your phone — the gyroscope, magnetic field, steps, gravity and altitude all feed your value while evolIA runs.
